title: Building Scalable AI Pipelines for Production
excerpt: A practical guide to designing machine learning pipelines that scale from prototype to production using modern MLOps practices.
content: When I first started building ML pipelines, I thought the hard part was the model. I was wrong. The hard part is everything after the model — the data plumbing, the monitoring, the retraining loop.

In this post, I'll walk through the architecture I've settled on after building production ML systems for the past three years. We'll cover data validation, feature stores, model versioning, and automated retraining.
Every ML pipeline needs three core stages: ingest, transform, and serve. The key is making each stage independently scalable and observable.
Use a message queue (Kafka or Redpanda) to decouple data sources from processing. This handles spikes, backfills, and schema evolution without downstream breakage.
A feature store (Feast or Tecton) centralizes feature computation so training and inference use identical logic. No more train-serve skew.
- Version everything: data, code, model artifacts
The result is a pipeline that runs reliably, scales horizontally, and alerts you before things break.
